P a g e  1 / 9  
 
1.  Introduction & Objectives 
The objective of Milestone-2 is to identify, evaluate, and prepare datasets required for 
training the perception and interaction modules of the navigation assistance system. 
The datasets used in this project support three primary components of the system: 
1. Object Detection Module – detects indoor obstacles and objects using RGB 
images. 
2. Depth Estimation Module – estimates distance to objects from monocular camera 
images.

images. 
3. Speech / TTS Module – converts navigation decisions into spoken instructions. 
 
The goal of this milestone is to ensure that the data used for these modules is: 
• Clearly sourced and legally usable 
• Properly structured and formatted 
• Sufficient in quantity and diversity 
• Free from significant quality issues 
• Prepared in a reproducible preprocessing pipeline 
• Split correctly to avoid data leakage 
 
1.1. Data-Specific Scope & Assumptions 
 
In-Scope Out-of-Scope

In-Scope Out-of-Scope 
Indoor environment datasets Outdoor navigation datasets 
Near-field obstacle detection (0–3 meters) Large-scale SLAM map datasets 
RGB camera-based perception Long-range depth estimation (>5 meters) 
Depth estimation from monocular or RGB-D 
datasets 
GPS-based navigation 
Speech synthesis datasets for navigation 
instructions

2. Object Detection Dataset Identification & Unification 
2.1. Target Class Taxonomy

2.1. Target Class Taxonomy 
To ensure the system remains focused on actionable indoor navigation, we 
identified 11 critical classes. These classes were selected based on their frequency 
in indoor environments and their status as potential navigational hazards or 
landmarks.
